WELLINGTON DEPOT

pages.raw_text:
Tne tracks to Wellington may be
zen ~ 40 days, but upon the ar
- q the county coroner, he has
idered all bodies taken out.


EpoT TURNED INTO MoReUE


Those bodies put away in tenn
prary Ice tombs have been uncov-
red and the little Wellington de-
pt Is so crowded with dead this
forming that survivors, diggers
nd newspapermen have accidental


stepped on discolored hand or
pot In getting in and out of the
telegraph office.


Seven undertakers are working
IL the time, and as soon as a body
\ embalmed it is wrapped in a
lanket and tagged. The little
Dom for the telegraph operator is
ne only place in the depot where
ne gruesome bundles are not piled


p, and every few minutes a new
orpse is dragged up from Death
alley.


The rescue work has gone on so
.everishly no one has taken time
% notice what else the avalanche
lid elsewhere. Great majestic
Washington pines were snapped off
ike matches, and in some cases
actually uprooted and carried on.
The slide created a new landscape
where the trains once stood.
4 BODIES OF FOUR CLERKS


It will be a week before all the
bodies are recovered. Half of them
are not even located. Twelve
bodies in all were unearthed yes
terday, but that was an unusually
nigh average. The body of Con-
ductor J. NJ Parzybok was found
last night, and a body previously
identified as his is now in the I'M
Identified list. Four unidentified
mail clerks were also found last
night.


ONE WHOLE FAMILY DEAD.


Sometimes one laughs that he
may not cry" said a philosopher.
We tried hard at Wellington yester-
day, but the laughs were mockery.
Though hardened now to the sight
of the dead, great, strong men
wept at the sight of the Beck FAM
lily. The father, mother, two daugh-
tiers and little son, lie side by side
in the morgue. The little baby's
face is crushed into an unrecogniz-
able mass.


TRAiNMEN CRY LIKE CHILDREN


Trainmen cried like children
when Blackburn's and Longcoy's
bodies were uncovered.


Ballots hotel, where everybody
Is living and which escaped the
first slide, is now pressed from the
rear by gigantic drift. A new slide
would undoubtedly wipe out the
town and everybody here, including
the injured in the temporary hos
pital.


No one here or on the trail to
Scenic is ever out of danger of
fresh slides. The soft snow or rain
here is constantly making new and
sticky drifts.


e A FORTUNE FOUND.


A. fortune has been found under
the avalanche among the wreckage
and the dead. Money orders and
registered mail from mail train No
81 and the personal effects of the
victims total 100000 The mail
sacks are being guarded by govern
ment men and the property of the
dead is being looked after by dep
uty sheriffs. There have been no
more attempts at footing since the
arrest of Roberts.


Enough food is being packed in
daily.
